question_text: What exotic metal is known for both exceptional chemical resistance and flammability characteristics?
explanation: Answer: A | Titanium is known for both exceptional chemical resistance (resistant to most acids and seawater) and for its flammability under certain conditions. Fine titanium powder, shavings, and turnings can ignite and burn with extreme intensity. This dual characteristic requires careful handling and cutting practices in fabrication.

question_text: Should stainless steel piping be stress-relieved after fabrication?
option_b: Yes — but only for large bore pipe
option_c: No — stress relief can sensitize stainless steel and destroy its corrosion resistance
explanation: Answer: C | Stainless steel should NOT be stress-relieved. Heating stainless steel in the temperature range of 425–850°C (793–1562°F) causes sensitization — carbon migrates to grain boundaries and combines with chromium, depleting the chromium from the grain boundary zone and making the steel susceptible to intergranular corrosion.

question_text: Why does the oil and gas industry prefer plastic piping over metal in many applications?
option_c: Resistance to wax buildup — crude oil deposits wax inside metal pipes while plastic has better resistance
explanation: Answer: C | The oil and gas industry uses plastic piping largely because crude oil and natural gas can deposit wax (paraffin) on the interior of metal pipes, restricting flow. Plastic pipe has better resistance to wax adhesion, reducing plugging frequency and maintenance costs in production and gathering systems.

question_text: What is the simplest way of joining fiberglass pipe and fittings?
explanation: Answer: B | The simplest method of joining FRP (fiberglass reinforced plastic) pipe is Hand Layup or the Contact Mould Method. Alternating strips of fiberglass mat saturated with catalyzed resin are applied over the joint by hand, building up layers of structural laminate. The method requires minimal equipment and no heat.

question_text: Name one advantage of using glass pipe in a piping system.
option_a: Corrosion Resistance — glass is chemically inert and resistant to virtually all acids bases and solvents
explanation: Answer: A | The primary advantage of glass pipe is its outstanding Corrosion Resistance. Glass is chemically inert and resistant to virtually all acids (except hydrofluoric acid), bases, solvents, and oxidizing agents. This makes it ideal for laboratory drain lines, pharmaceutical processes, and chemical plants handling aggressive fluids.

question_text: What is the name for the strands formed by spinning a quantity of glass fibers together?
explanation: Answer: C | Rovings are continuous bundles of glass filaments formed by gathering and lightly twisting many individual glass fiber strands together. They are used as the primary structural reinforcement in filament-wound fiberglass pipe. Rovings can be used as-is or chopped for spray-up applications.

question_text: Kimax is what type of pipe?
option_c: Glass pipe — borosilicate glass pipe with thermal and chemical resistance
explanation: Answer: C | Kimax is a brand of borosilicate Glass pipe. Borosilicate glass (also used in Pyrex) has a very low coefficient of thermal expansion and excellent thermal shock resistance. Kimax pipe is used in laboratory drain systems, pharmaceutical manufacturing, and chemical processes requiring maximum chemical purity and transparency.

question_text: What post-weld treatment is required on nickel alloys to maintain their corrosion resistance after welding?
option_c: Passivating — restoring the protective oxide layer
explanation: Answer: C | Passivating is required after welding nickel alloys. The heat of welding disturbs the protective oxide layer on nickel alloy surfaces, leaving them susceptible to corrosion. Chemical passivation (using appropriate acid solutions) restores the passive film and returns the metal to its design corrosion resistance.

question_text: How is the strongest type of fiberglass pipe constructed?
option_d: Filament Winding — continuous fibers wound at precise angles under tension around a mandrel
explanation: Answer: D | Filament Wound fiberglass pipe is the strongest type. Continuous glass fiber rovings are wound under controlled tension at precise angles (typically ±55° for pressure pipe) around a rotating mandrel with resin applied simultaneously. This creates a highly oriented fiber structure with maximum strength-to-weight ratio.

question_text: What safety equipment must always be worn when working with MEKP?
explanation: Answer: B | Monogoggles (chemical splash goggles providing a complete seal around the eyes) must always be worn when working with MEKP. MEKP is a strong oxidizer and organic peroxide that causes severe and potentially permanent eye damage on contact. Standard safety glasses do not provide adequate splash protection.

question_text: Can lined pipe be welded?
option_a: Yes — lined pipe can be welded provided the liner integrity is maintained
option_c: No — welding always destroys the liner
option_d: Only by cutting out and replacing the liner section
explanation: Answer: A | Lined pipe can be welded, provided care is taken not to damage the liner during the process. Typically the liner is pulled back from the weld zone, the pipe is welded, and then the liner is reinstalled or new liner material is applied at the joint. Some liners (like PTFE) use special insert fittings that are not affected by welding.

question_text: What chemical corrodes and attacks glass pipe?
explanation: Answer: D | Hydrofluoric Acid (HF) is one of the very few chemicals that can dissolve and attack glass. HF reacts with the silicon dioxide in glass to form silicon tetrafluoride gas, etching or dissolving the glass surface. This makes glass pipe completely incompatible with any HF service — PTFE or specialized plastic piping must be used instead.

question_text: What is another name for catalysts used in the fiberglassing process?
explanation: Answer: A | Catalysts used in fiberglass work are also called Hardeners. They are added to the polyester or epoxy resin to initiate and accelerate the chemical cross-linking (curing) reaction that converts the liquid resin to a solid. The most common catalyst for polyester resin is MEKP (methyl ethyl ketone peroxide).

question_text: If one doubles the amount of catalyst added to FRP resin how does that affect the gel time?
option_b: Reduces the gel time by one half
option_c: Has no effect on gel time
explanation: Answer: B | Doubling the catalyst (MEKP) concentration reduces the gel time by approximately one half. The catalyst initiates the free-radical polymerization reaction; more catalyst means more initiation sites, accelerating the reaction and causing the resin to gel in half the normal time. Temperature also greatly affects gel time — higher temperatures speed curing.

question_text: Why is silicon added to cast iron during its production?
explanation: Answer: B | Silicon is added to cast iron primarily to improve its Corrosion Resistance. High-silicon cast iron (Duriron, 14.5% Si) is highly resistant to sulfuric acid and many other corrosive chemicals. Silicon also promotes the formation of graphite flakes and improves castability during production.

question_text: Can PE (polyethylene) pipe be solvent welded?
option_b: No — PE cannot be solvent welded and must be joined by fusion welding or mechanical fittings
explanation: Answer: B | Polyethylene (PE) pipe cannot be solvent welded. Its chemical resistance to solvents — one of its key advantages — makes it immune to the dissolving action of solvent cements. PE must be joined by butt fusion welding, electrofusion fittings, or approved mechanical compression or flange fittings.

question_text: What type of valve is mounted between flanges and held in place entirely by the flange bolts?
explanation: Answer: C | A Wafer Type valve is designed to sit between two pipe flanges with no integral flanges of its own. It is held in position and sealed entirely by the flange bolts that clamp through the flanges on each side of the valve. This design is much more compact and lightweight than a traditional flanged valve.

question_text: What hazards might a fitter encounter when working on a demineralizer?
option_b: Caustic and Acid — the regeneration chemicals are highly corrosive
explanation: Answer: B | When working on a demineralizer (ion exchange water treatment unit), a fitter may encounter Caustic (sodium hydroxide, NaOH) and Acid (typically hydrochloric or sulfuric acid) used to regenerate the cation and anion exchange resins respectively. These chemicals are highly corrosive and require full PPE including face shield, chemical-resistant gloves, and protective clothing.

question_text: When using the hand lay-up method of joining FRP pipe which strip must be applied first?
option_b: The Narrow Strip — placed at the joint centerline to create the initial bond
explanation: Answer: B | In the hand lay-up method, the Narrow Strip is applied first at the joint centerline. This initial narrow strip bridges the two pipe ends and creates the first structural bond across the joint gap. Successively wider strips are then applied over it to build up the required laminate thickness and structural strength.

question_text: When working with fiberglass and using MEKP catalyst what should never be added directly to it?
option_c: A Promoter (accelerator) — mixing promoter directly with MEKP creates an explosive reaction
explanation: Answer: C | A Promoter (cobalt naphthenate) must NEVER be added directly to MEKP catalyst. The combination of these two chemicals in concentrated form creates a violently exothermic reaction that can cause fire or explosion. The correct procedure is to add promoter to the resin first, mix thoroughly, then add the MEKP separately.

question_text: What is the primary element that gives stainless steel its corrosion resistance?
explanation: Answer: A | Chromium is the primary alloying element responsible for stainless steel's corrosion resistance. When present at 10.5% or more by weight, chromium reacts with oxygen to form a thin, stable, self-repairing chromium oxide passive layer on the surface that prevents further oxidation and corrosion.

question_text: The process of forming a protective oxide layer on the surface of stainless steel is called what?
explanation: Answer: C | Passivating is the chemical treatment process that promotes and strengthens the natural chromium oxide passive layer on stainless steel surfaces. After fabrication or welding, the passive layer may be disturbed or contaminated. Passivation (typically using nitric or citric acid) cleans the surface and re-establishes the protective layer.

question_text: What material is the least expensive type of pipe liner?
explanation: Answer: C | Polypropylene is the least expensive pipe liner material. It offers good chemical resistance to acids, bases, and most solvents at moderate temperatures, making it the cost-effective choice for many chemical service applications where a more expensive liner material is not required.

question_text: How often must a glass piping system be supported along its length?
option_d: At every change of direction only
explanation: Answer: B | A glass piping system must be supported At Every Joint. Glass is brittle and has low tensile and bending strength — it cannot span the distances that metal pipe can without excessive deflection and stress. Supporting at every joint provides the close spacing needed to prevent breakage from the pipe's own weight.

question_text: Stainless pipe is made from what metals?
explanation: Answer: B | Stainless steel pipe is made from Chromium, Nickel, and Iron. The chromium content (typically 18%) provides corrosion resistance via the passive oxide layer, nickel (typically 8-10% in austenitic grades) adds toughness, formability, and high-temperature strength, and iron forms the structural base.

question_text: Stainless steel is an alloy made of what metals?
explanation: Answer: B | Stainless steel is an alloy of Nickel, Chromium, and Iron as its primary constituents. The chromium (minimum 10.5%) provides corrosion resistance through the passive oxide layer, nickel improves toughness, ductility, and high-temperature performance, and iron is the base metal. Other elements (molybdenum, manganese) are added in specific grades.

question_text: Why is specialty purpose piping needed in industrial applications?
option_d: For corrosion prevention where standard carbon steel cannot handle corrosive chemicals
explanation: Answer: D | Special purpose piping is required for Corrosion Prevention. In chemical plants, pulp mills, and other industries, many process fluids are highly corrosive to standard carbon steel. Specialty materials such as stainless steel, lined pipe, FRP, plastic, and exotic alloys provide the chemical compatibility and corrosion resistance needed.

question_text: What is the main contaminant found in surface water that must be treated before use?
explanation: Answer: C | The main contaminant in surface water is Suspended Solids — particles of silt, clay, organic matter, algae, and debris carried in the water. These must be removed by coagulation, settling, and filtration before the water can be used in process systems, boilers, or potable water applications.

question_text: A substance composed of two or more metals combined together is called what?
explanation: Answer: B | An Alloy is a metallic material composed of two or more elements, at least one of which is a metal. Alloys are created to produce properties superior to those of the individual elements — such as greater strength, corrosion resistance, or specific electrical properties. Stainless steel, brass, and bronze are common alloys.

question_text: Can PVC pipe be fusion welded?
option_a: No — PVC cannot be welded at all
option_d: Yes — PVC can be fusion welded using heat to melt and fuse the joint
explanation: Answer: D | Yes, PVC pipe can be fusion welded using heat. Unlike polyethylene which requires butt fusion or electrofusion, PVC can also be joined by solvent cement. However, fusion welding of PVC is used in specific applications where solvent cementing is not appropriate, using heating tools to melt and fuse the pipe ends together.

question_text: Elastomer pipe liners are made from what natural material?
option_d: Rubber — natural or synthetic rubber bonded to the pipe interior
explanation: Answer: D | Elastomer pipe liners are made from Rubber — natural or synthetic elastomers bonded or compressed into the interior of a metal pipe. The rubber liner provides excellent chemical resistance, erosion resistance, and resilience against particle impact, protecting the pipe wall from corrosive or abrasive process fluids.

question_text: What is the most exotic and expensive piping material in use today?
explanation: Answer: D | Titanium is the most exotic and expensive piping material in common industrial use. Its exceptional corrosion resistance (comparable to platinum in many environments), high strength-to-weight ratio, and biocompatibility make it invaluable in highly aggressive chemical service, offshore oil production, and specialized aerospace applications despite its very high cost.
